justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as the first escalation with detailed options. Clock-start ambiguity identified in assumptions and edge cases. Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6 and escalated with adjudication concerns. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted in escalation #3 (overlapping refund triggers). 10 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications. 4 formal escalations provided. Confidence level of 38 is well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal contradiction is the lead escalation with three resolution options and a concrete recommendation — exactly the kind of domain authority Bird should show
Confidence at 38 with thorough reasoning about why it's low, plus well-separated high/low confidence areas
10 business rules extracted — double the minimum — with thoughtful invariant classifications (only 10km radius marked as true invariant)
Edge cases are exceptional — the driver-cancellation-at-minute-119 scenario and the double-cancellation scenario show genuine domain reasoning
Wrong-address adjudication escalated as its own item with prevention-first recommendation (address validation at order time)
Clock-start assumption explicitly stated: '2-hour window starts at order placement, not at driver pickup' — correctly flags this as an assumption rather than a resolved fact

Clock-start ambiguity could have been elevated to a formal escalation rather than buried in assumptions — the PM vs Ops disagreement on when the clock starts is a stakeholder conflict, not just an assumption
Ubiquitous language section is well-constructed with precise definitions, especially the distinction between 'Goodwill Refund' (non-contractual) which itself encodes the Legal position

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'ambiguity', 'ambiguity', 'missing_context'
